gpt_review.biasedText: It seems almost ghoulish to look for a silver lining in the dark cloud that blanketed the nation last week.
gpt_review.reason: The phrasing is vivid and emotive; while I did not flag it, it reasonably counts as dramatic, tone-setting language.

gpt_review.biasedText: ‘National conversations’ are usually efforts to bully everyone into accepting a single narrative when the reality is that, in this country of more than 300 million, many narratives can be in conflict and still be legitimate.
gpt_review.reason: This is a sweeping characterization presented assertively; defensible as opinion-as-fact even though I did not flag it.
Pejorative descriptor of opponents; a reasonable catch for subjective labeling.

gpt_review.biasedText: That's true, and tragic, and fairly irrelevant.
gpt_review.reason: Author’s evaluative judgment framed declaratively; defensible as opinion presented as fact.

gpt_review.biasedText: Is it really so unfathomable that African American citizens should be outraged or distrustful of government when they have good reason to believe the state is murdering young black men?
gpt_review.reason: Emotionally charged phrasing (“state is murdering”) adds rhetorical force; a reasonable spin detection.

gpt_review.biasedText: Liberals, for their part, are often rather obtuse in how they discuss policing.
gpt_review.reason: “Obtuse” is a pejorative, subjective label for a political group; reasonably identified as biased language.

gpt_review.biasedText: creating one of the strangest ironies of modern liberalism
gpt_review.reason: Subjective characterization asserted as a general truth; a defensible opinion-as-fact flag.

gpt_review.biasedText: It's particularly amazing given that law enforcement personnel typically receive far more training than your typical bureaucrat or legislator.
gpt_review.reason: “Amazing” signals personal evaluation and surprise; reasonable to classify as spin.
